channel.title: Algorithms on Just Another Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Algorithms on Just Another Blog

item.title: Merge-sort footprint
item.description: Background Implementation Memory Footprint Background Every book on algorithms and data-structures covers merge-sort algorithms. It has an \( O(N\times logN) \) time complexity and \( O(N) \) space complexity. But there are two interesting properties I want to explore which I haven’t seen covered: recursion depth and memory footprint. So I first write my own merge-sort, and hook up some counters and gauges for tracking:

item.title: Comparison Sorting
item.description: Benchmark Implementation I wanted to compare just how fast or slow an \( O(N^2) \) sorting algorithm is compared to a \( O(N \times logN) \) one. So here are the results that I found. Benchmark BubbleSort is ridiculously slow, so much infact that in our first graph it’s hard to distinguish rest of the algorithms.
